---
name: Filled IDENTITY Review Request
about: Share your filled IDENTITY.md for community review (optional, public)
title: "[IDENTITY-REVIEW] "
labels: identity-review
assignees: ''
---

> **Note:** This is opt-in only. The scaffold ships with `IDENTITY.example.md` (fictional Jordan Chen) deliberately so users can fill their own privately. Some adopters find it useful to share a filled IDENTITY publicly for review or comparison — this template gives that a structured home.

## Why you're sharing

<!--
- "I'd like a sanity check on my axioms before committing them as load-bearing"
- "I think my section on $TOPIC is novel and worth discussing"
- "I'm comparing notes on what mature Layer 1 files look like"
-->

## Your filled IDENTITY (paste or link)

<!--
Either paste the relevant sections inline OR link to a public gist /
your own repo where the file lives. If linking, please use a permalink
(commit-pinned URL) so the conversation references a stable version.
-->

```markdown
# Paste your IDENTITY here or link below
```

**Link to source:**

## Specific feedback you want

- [ ] Are my axioms actually convictions, or aspirations? (the most common Layer 1 mistake)
- [ ] Does my "What I've changed my mind about" section reveal real thinking texture?
- [ ] Are there contradictions or vagueness I missed?
- [ ] Does my Layer 1 set up Layer 2 well — would my rules feel like they descend from this?
- [ ] Other:

## How long has this been in use

<!-- Days / weeks / months. Tells reviewers how settled vs aspirational this is. -->

## What would you change about the scaffold based on writing this?

<!--
Optional but valuable. Did the scaffold's prompts get in the way?
Were there sections you skipped or expanded? Feedback here helps
improve the scaffold itself.
-->

---

By sharing here, you agree your IDENTITY content is public-licensable
(or already is, if you've linked an MIT/permissive source). The
maintainer reserves the right to use anonymized observations from
review threads to improve the scaffold, but will not lift identifying
content without permission.
